stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags including 'transformers' and 'llama'.
The content is relevant to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in generative AI tasks.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in large language model applications.
The tags confirm its relevance within the transformers and LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: China may have accessed Mythos
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: US export restrictions on Anthropic's Mythos are reportedly linked to national security concerns.
The fear stems from potential unauthorized access to Mythos by a group associated with China.
If Chinese entities accessed Mythos 5 or Fable 5, it would constitute a serious national security risk.

stories.title: Amazon security research reportedly led to the White House’s Anthropic Fable ban
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Amazon's cybersecurity research reportedly influenced a White House directive.
This directive led to Anthropic restricting access to its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models.
The event highlights the intersection of corporate security research, government policy, and advanced AI deployment.

stories.title: PeopleSoft 0-day affecting hundreds of organizations steals gigabytes of data
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A critical 0-day vulnerability exists in PeopleSoft software, which is owned by Oracle.
The exploit allows unauthorized access, leading to the theft of gigabytes of sensitive data.
The vulnerability affects hundreds of organizations, indicating widespread risk across various industries.

stories.title: Ukraine's one-time test used fully autonomous drones to kill Russian soldiers
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Ukraine has demonstrated the use of fully autonomous drones in combat operations against Russian soldiers.
The report highlights Ukraine's integration of AI modules onto drones and robots.
The deployment signifies a move toward advanced, autonomous military technology in the conflict.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on the Hugging Face platform.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The associated pipeline is focused on text-generation tasks.
Key technologies involved include transformers, PyTorch, and safetensors.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for building personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It integrates built-in support for Ollama, a popular local LLM runner.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance optimization.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, enhancing local AI inference speed.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, emphasizing on-device processing for privacy and speed.
The core value proposition is connecting messaging apps to local AI capabilities running entirely on the user's hardware.

stories.title: Anthropic shuts down Fable, Mythos models following Trump admin directive
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: Anthropic has discontinued the Fable and Mythos models.
The shutdown was reportedly due to a directive from the Trump administration.
The Commerce Department expressed concern that a Fable 5 'jailbreak' posed a national security risk.

stories.title: ClinHallu: A Benchmark for Diagnosing Stage-Wise Hallucinations in Medical MLLM Reasoning
stories.author: Lei Zhu
bullet_points: Introduces ClinHallu, a new benchmark specifically designed to diagnose stage-wise hallucinations in medical Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs).

Addresses a critical gap in current medical hallucination research by focusing on the *source* of errors (e.g., visual misrecognition, knowledge recall, reasoning integration) rather than just data collection.
Highlights the importance of building trustworthy MLLMs for reliable clinical decision support in healthcare.

stories.title: When Good Verifiers Go Bad: Self-Improving VLMs Can Regress on New Tasks
stories.author: Jianzhe Lin
bullet_points: The paper investigates the reliability of verifier-driven self-improvement in Visual-Language Models (VLMs) using Self-DPO.
It challenges the common deployment assumption that a stronger verifier always leads to a stronger student model.
The research demonstrates that verifier quality is highly task-specific, causing performance regressions when applied across different tasks (e.g., MathVista).

stories.title: ClinHallu: A Benchmark for Diagnosing Stage-Wise Hallucinations in Medical MLLM Reasoning
stories.author: Lei Zhu
bullet_points: Introduces ClinHallu, a new benchmark designed to diagnose stage-wise hallucinations in medical Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs).
Addresses a critical gap in current benchmarks by focusing on the *source* of hallucinations (e.g., visual misrecognition, knowledge recall errors, flawed reasoning) rather than just data collection.
Highlights the importance of building trustworthy MLLMs for reliable clinical decision support in healthcare.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon using its MLX engine.
The performance improvements result in faster response times and reduced memory usage for models.
The updates also lead to higher quality outputs from the language models running on the platform.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release introduces performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving capabilities on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It supports running various coding models (e.g., Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports experimental local image generation on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models entirely on their local machines.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Under-16 social media ban announced by UK government
stories.author: Dominic Preston
bullet_points: The UK government is implementing a total social media ban for individuals under the age of 16.
This policy follows a similar move by Australia and is set to take effect potentially in early next year.
The ban will be complemented by measures restricting online interactions with strangers in games and livestreaming.

stories.title: SpaceX is now a public company valued for its AI potential, so what comes next?
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: SpaceX has transitioned to a publicly valued entity, driven by investor expectations.
The valuation is heavily tied to the potential of SpaceX's Artificial Intelligence capabilities.
The article prompts discussion on the future trajectory of the company given its new financial structure.

stories.title: Learning Coordinated Preference for Multi-Objective Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Jingdi Chen
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of Cooperative Multi-Objective Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MOMARL) where objectives can conflict.
Proposes Preference Coordinated Multi-agent Policy Optimization (PCMA) to handle conflicts across objectives and agents.
Aims to enable complementary trade-offs among agents by learning coordinated, agent-specific preferences.

stories.title: Towards Direct Latent-Space Synthesis for Parallel Branches in LLM-Agent Workflows
stories.author: Pan Li
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of LLMs processing structured, parallel agent workflows via sequential text input.
Proposes 'Direct Latent-Space Synthesis' to handle multiple independent agent branches efficiently.
Aims to overcome the inefficiency of current methods, which concatenate textual outputs and cause redundant prefill.

stories.title: Giving AI a Headache: Acoustic Adversarial Attacks to Computer Vision Applications
stories.author: Michael Teti
bullet_points: Acoustic adversarial attacks can disrupt computer vision systems used in real-world applications like autonomous vehicles.
The attacks induce physical motion in cameras via acoustic vibrations, overwhelming internal stabilization mechanisms.
This induced motion causes artifacts in the captured frames, leading to system failure or misinterpretation.

stories.title: Abstracting Cross-Domain Action Sequences into Interpretable Workflows
stories.author: Scott Counts
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of extracting meaningful insights from noisy, granular user interaction logs.
It proposes a method to abstract cross-domain action sequences into interpretable workflows, moving beyond simple action clustering.
The work aims to improve digital products by grounding design decisions in real-world user behavior while improving generalization across applications.

stories.title: Listening with Attention: Entropy-Guided Explainability for Transformer-Based Audio Models
stories.author: Agoritsa Polyzou
bullet_points: Addresses the interpretability challenge in high-accuracy Transformer-based Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) models like Whisper.
Introduces LEAF-X, a novel, model-intrinsic Explainable AI (XAI) framework for ASR.
LEAF-X utilizes entropy-guided attention weighting and multi-layer attention rollout to provide faithful and temporally grounded explanations.

stories.title: From Self-Supervised Speech Models to Mixture-of-Experts for Robust Anti-Spoofing
stories.author: Mickael Rouvier
bullet_points: Addresses the growing challenge of anti-spoofing due to highly natural synthetic speech.
Proposes transforming a self-supervised speech model into a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture.
The MoE approach aims to significantly improve the generalization and robustness of spoofing detection systems against unseen synthesis methods.

stories.title: Gaze Heads: How VLMs Look at What They Describe
stories.author: David Bau
bullet_points: The research identifies a specific internal mechanism in Vision-Language Models (VLMs) responsible for image description.
This mechanism, termed 'gaze heads,' consists of attention heads that track the image region the model is currently describing.
The discovery was validated using a controlled testbed of comic strips to map narrative order to spatial attention.

stories.title: Persona-Pruner: Sculpting Lightweight Models for Role-Playing
stories.author: Jongheon Jeong
bullet_points: Addresses the computational inefficiency of using large, generalist Language Models for specific personas.
Introduces 'Persona-Pruner,' a method to create lightweight models tailored for specific roles.
Relevant to scaling AI applications, particularly in complex, multi-agent systems like role-playing ecosystems.

stories.title: Compressed Computation is (probably) not Computation in Superposition
stories.author: Stefan Heimersheim
bullet_points: The research investigates whether the Compressed Computation (CC) toy model qualifies as computation in superposition.
The CC model demonstrates efficiency by computing 100 ReLU functions using only 50 neurons, outperforming expectations.
The study attributes this performance to an unintended input mixing via a noisy residual stream, which is analyzed by splitting the training objective.

stories.title: Gaze Heads: How VLMs Look at What They Describe
stories.author: David Bau
bullet_points: The research introduces 'gaze heads,' a specific mechanism within Vision-Language Models (VLMs) that tracks which part of an image the model is currently describing.
These gaze heads are identified using a simple correlation score during forward passes, demonstrating an internal attentional focus.
The study uses comic strips as a controlled environment to test how the model spatially aligns its description with the narrative order.

stories.title: Persona-Pruner: Sculpting Lightweight Models for Role-Playing
stories.author: Jongheon Jeong
bullet_points: Addresses the computational inefficiency of using large, generalist Language Models for specific, role-playing personas.
Proposes 'Persona-Pruner,' a method to sculpt lightweight models tailored for individual character roles.
Relevant for applications requiring numerous, simultaneous, stylized interactions (e.g., complex NPC ecosystems).

stories.title: AdaSR: Adaptive Streaming Reasoning with Hierarchical Relative Policy Optimization
stories.author: Xiaoyu Shen
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitations of traditional 'read-then-think' paradigms in large reasoning models.
It focuses on enabling models to perform reasoning while processing continuous, dynamic data streams (like audio/video).
The proposed method, AdaSR, introduces Adaptive Streaming Reasoning using Hierarchical Relative Policy Optimization.

stories.title: CORA: Analyzing and bridging thinking-answer gap in Multimodal RLVR via Consistency-Oriented Reasoning Alignment
stories.author: Nayu Liu
bullet_points: Addresses the 'thinking-answer gap' in Multimodal Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR).
Focuses on semantic inconsistency between the reasoning process and the final output, a gap often overlooked by existing methods.
Extends RLVR techniques to multimodal scenarios, aiming to improve reasoning alignment in large vision-language models.

stories.title: AgentSpec: Understanding Embodied Agent Scaffolds Through Controlled Composition
stories.author: Lianhui Qin
bullet_points: AgentSpec introduces a modular specification framework for embodied LLM agents.
It addresses the challenge of tightly coupled pipelines in complex agent systems (reasoning, memory, action, etc.).
The framework allows researchers to isolate component contributions and compare different agent designs.

stories.title: Towards Direct Latent-Space Synthesis for Parallel Branches in LLM-Agent Workflows
stories.author: Pan Li
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of sequential text processing in LLM-agent workflows.
Proposes 'Direct Latent-Space Synthesis' to handle parallel subtasks in agent systems.
Aims to overcome inefficiencies caused by concatenating textual outputs from independent branches.

stories.title: AI Doesn't Hallucinate. Your Architecture Does.
stories.author: raphink
bullet_points: The article reframes LLM 'hallucination' not as a bug, but as an inherent mechanism of the models.
It argues the core issue lies in the misallocation of non-determinism within the system design.
It critiques simplistic solutions, suggesting that documentation alone ('SKILLS.md') is insufficient for solving these architectural problems.

stories.title: Loop Engineering: The Next Step After Prompt Engineering for AI Agents
stories.author: mininglamp
bullet_points: The article discusses 'Loop Engineering' as a potential evolution beyond current prompt engineering techniques.
This concept is specifically framed as the next stage in developing sophisticated AI agents.
The topic suggests a shift in AI development focus towards iterative, closed-loop systems.
